channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: USC up to No. 4 in women's AP Top 25 after win over UConn. UCLA, South Carolina, Notre Dame stay 1-3
item.description: Southern California jumped to No. 4 in The Associated Press women's college basketball poll on Monday after edging UConn. The Trojans moved up three spots in the AP Top 25 after beating the then-No. 4 Huskies 72-70 on Saturday night in a rematch of last season's Elite Eight game that UConn won.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Shohei Ohtani wins 3rd AP Male Athlete of the Year award, tying Michael Jordan for 1 shy of record
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is keeping elite company. The Japanese superstar caps 2024 by winning The Associated Press Male Athlete of the Year for the third time, tying him with basketball great Michael Jordan. He trails only four-time winners Lance Armstrong, Tiger Woods and LeBron James.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Andrew McCutchen signs 1-year, $5-million deal to stay with the Pittsburgh Pirates, AP source says
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Andrew McCutchen is sticking around to see if the Pittsburgh Pirates can finish the climb back to contention.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sean Manaea to re-sign with Mets for $75 million over 3 years, AP source says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean Manaea is set to return to the New York Mets on a $75 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the agreement told The Associated Press. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity Monday because the deal was subject to a successful physical.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mickey, Minnie, Goofy and Wemby: Spurs-Knicks Christmas game is also an animated one at Disney World
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a Christmas Day basketball game at Walt Disney World, featuring Mickey, Minnie, Goofy and Wemby. An animated game, anyway.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tyreek Hill says drops against 49ers were a result of lack of practice reps with Tua Tagovailoa
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Tyreek Hill has not shied away from accountability during one of the least productive seasons of his nine-year career.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WNBA star takes aim at Elon Musk, rips billionaires after funding bill gets passed
item.description: Phoenix Mercury star Natasha Cloud took aim at Elon Musk and ripped billionaires in a post on X after the short-term stopgap government funding bill was passed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Liverpool routs Spurs 6-3 to take 4-point lead into Christmas in Premier League. Man United humbled
item.description: Liverpool heads into Christmas with a four-point lead, a game in hand and as the overwhelming title favorite in the Premier League on the back of a wild 6-3 win at Tottenham on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thunder sign defensive guard Alex Caruso to multi-year extension
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — The Oklahoma City Thunder announced Sunday that they have signed Alex Caruso to a multi-year extension. Caruso’s agent, Greg Lawrence, confirmed ESPN’s report that it is a four-year, $81 million deal.
item.dc:creator: CLIFF BRUNT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penix wins debut as Atlanta QB as Falcons return two picks for TDs, beat hapless Giants 34-7
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Michael Penix Jr. felt a brief bit of apprehension as he ran down the tunnel, which was to be expected. Then he played like a veteran for the Atlanta Falcons.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: College Football Playoffs looks for good matchups after snoozy first round of blowouts
item.description: For those who dozed through it, or flipped to more compelling fare in the NFL, here's a brief recap of the rollout of the much-anticipated College Football Playoff. Average final score: Winners 36, Losers 17.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NBA fines Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla $35,000 for postgame comments toward officials
item.description: The NBA fined the Boston Celtics coach $35,000 on Saturday “for aggressively pursuing and directing inappropriate language toward a game official” at the end of his team's loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Guenther scores twice as Utah HC beats Minnesota, 2-1
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is in seventh heaven right about now. Dylan Guenther scored twice, including the game-winner at 10:01 of the third period, as Utah ran its road winning streak to seven in a row Friday night with a hard-fought 2-1 victory over the Minnesota Wild.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ducks to honor fellow O.C. team at promotional night
item.description: If you're a fan of Orange County sports, you're in luck this January. On Jan. 7, the Anaheim Ducks are hosting Angels Night, an event that includes a baseball jersey giveaway for the first 5,000 fans in attendance. The game against the Calgary Flames begins at 7 p.m. Tickets begin at less than $30.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Trevor Baskin: From Division II star to Division I grinder
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — From Division II standout to Division I contributor, Trevor Baskin’s basketball journey has been anything but ordinary. After excelling at Colorado Mesa University, Baskin now competes at the highest level of college basketball with the Colorado Buffaloes.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Boston's Sports Bar & Restaurant's 'Gather Your Herd' Sweepstakes
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — With the NFL playoffs quickly approaching, Boston’s Pizza is giving six lucky fans a chance to celebrate Denver’s postseason push at the ultimate football watch party. “We’ve got some really nice TVs in our bar,” said Brandon Cramer, Boston’s Pizza general manager.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: As Caleb Williams gains experience, Bears must find meaning in final 3 games of dismal 2024 season
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — The Chicago Bears continue to get the same result regardless of how they approach the assignment. Quarterback Caleb Williams looked physically and mentally beaten at the end of Monday night’s 30-12 loss to the Minnesota Vikings, the eighth straight Bears defeat.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox change name of ballpark to Rate Field
item.description: CHICAGO — The Chicago White Sox have changed the name of Guaranteed Rate Field to Rate Field. The name change is because the sponsor of the ballpark, Guaranteed Rate, changed their name to Rate.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Runnin' Utes roll past Florida A&M, 89-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah's last home game of 2024 was a memorable one, as the Utes cruised past Florida A&M, 89-59. Mike Sharavjamts and Mason Madsen each scored 15 points off the bench to lead a balanced Utah attack. The Utes bench scored 57 points, while the starters scored 32.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Donato caps Blackhawks comeback, Blackhawks top Capitals 3-2
item.description: Ryan Donato scored his team-leading 11th goal with 2:18 left in regulation to cap Chicago's three-goal, third-period rally and lift the Blackhawks to a 3-2 win over the Washington Capitals on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Column: The Chicago Bears have no fire in the trenches
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — When it comes to what's wrong with the Chicago Bears, some argue it's coaching. Others dare to say it's Caleb Williams. Most toss it up to organizational dysfunction, or some supernatural hex inadvertently brought about by the McCaskeys being the McCaskeys.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Girls Soccer: Our Lady of Peace 6, Patrick Henry 3
item.description: Holiday Jubilee tournament in girls soccer OLP facing off against Patrick Henry. OLP took home the 6-3 victory.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: NBA unveils new All-Star format featuring four-team tournament
item.description: The NBA 's All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing on Tuesday that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rays call for more money after Pinellas commissioners vote in favor of bonds for new stadium
item.description: Pinellas County commissioners voted in favor of approving bonds for the new Tampa Bay Rays stadium Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Isaac Wilson decides to return to Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Upon second thought, Isaac Wilson is returning to Utah. Eight days after announcing he was entering the transfer portal, Wilson announced that he is returning to Utah, where will compete for the starting quarterback job with New Mexico transfer Devon Dampier.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs with Bowles on team's December rise and a dominating win against the Chargers
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers continued to add pressure in the NFC South title race with a commanding 40-17 win over the Los Angeles Chargers.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC
